rs8176719.description: The 261G deletion. 'D' represents the deletion (O allele), 'I' represents the insertion (A/B allele).

rs8176747.description: The 803G>C mutation. The 'C' allele is specific to the B blood group.

rs590787.description: A proxy marker for the RHD gene deletion. Rh-negative status is recessive.

rs429358.name: APOE ε4 marker 1
rs429358.description: APOE gene variant associated with Alzheimer's risk

rs7412.name: APOE ε4 marker 2
rs7412.description: Second APOE variant - combined with rs429358 determines ε2/ε3/ε4 status

rs1333049.name: 9p21 CAD risk
rs1333049.description: Coronary artery disease risk variant on chromosome 9

rs7903146.name: TCF7L2
rs7903146.description: Strongest known genetic risk factor for Type 2 diabetes

rs6025.name: Factor V Leiden
rs6025.description: Increased risk of blood clots (DVT, PE)

rs33930165.name: Beta Thalassemia / HbE Marker
rs33930165.description: HBB variant causing Hemoglobin E, associated with mild beta thalassemia or hemolytic anemia

rs33950507.name: Beta Thalassemia splice-site mutation
rs33950507.description: Common Mediterranean HBB mutation (IVS-I-110 G>A) causing beta-plus thalassemia

rs11886868.name: BCL11A Thalassemia modifier
rs11886868.description: BCL11A variant that regulates fetal hemoglobin (HbF) persistence, ameliorating thalassemia severity

source.title: Common Kibra Alleles Are Associated with Human Memory Performance
source.finding: VKORC1 -1639G>A: AA genotype requires ~50% lower warfarin dose.
source.authors: Papassotiropoulos A, Stephan DA, Huentelman MJ, et al.

CC.effect: Homozygous for the C allele (risk allele). Associated with lower episodic memory performance, reduced hippocampal activation during memory retrieval tasks.

CT.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Intermediate memory performance with moderate hippocampal activation.

TC.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Intermediate memory performance with moderate hippocampal activation.

TT.effect: Homozygous for the T allele (reference/favorable allele). Associated with enhanced episodic memory performance, higher hippocampal activation during memory retrieval tasks.

AA.effect: Associated with lower bone mineral density and altered vitamin D metabolism.
AA.recommendation: Monitor bone density. Ensure vitamin D and calcium sufficiency.

AA.effect: Associated with altered vitamin D signaling efficiency.
AA.recommendation: Monitor vitamin D levels. May need higher supplementation.

GG.effect: DPYD HapB3 tag homozygous. Complete DPD deficiency. Life-threatening fluoropyrimidine toxicity.
GG.recommendation: Avoid fluoropyrimidines entirely. Use alternative chemotherapy.

CG.effect: DPYD HapB3 tag heterozygous. Partial DPD deficiency with increased toxicity risk.
CG.recommendation: Reduce fluoropyrimidine dose by at least 50%. Close monitoring required.

GC.effect: DPYD HapB3 tag heterozygous. Partial DPD deficiency with increased toxicity risk.
GC.recommendation: Reduce fluoropyrimidine dose by at least 50%. Close monitoring required.

CC.effect: No HapB3 variant. Normal DPD activity.

phenotype_interpretation.rapid_acetylator: Diplotype with functional NAT2 alleles (*4/*4). Population frequency 5-10% (higher near equator). Normal to rapid isoniazid acetylation.
phenotype_interpretation.intermediate_acetylator: One functional and one reduced-function allele (*4/*5, *4/*6, *4/*7). Population frequency 40-60%. Intermediate acetylation capacity.
phenotype_interpretation.slow_acetylator: Two reduced-function alleles (*5/*5, *5/*6, *5/*7, *6/*6, *6/*7, *7/*7). Population frequency 10-80% (variable by ancestry). Slow acetylation with drug accumulation and increased hepatotoxicity risk.
